codeReview.summary: I reviewed the prior failed report, both feature handoffs, both feature diffs (`2b33da7` and `d8704f6`), the fix transcript skeleton, and the current typecheck/codegen paths. The fix does address the original undefined-binding bug for the specific one-level nested Copy-payload cases it tests (`Option<Option<_>>` and `Result<Option<_>>`). But the combined original+fix implementation is still not correct for all accepted nested built-in patterns: the new codegen path is only one layer deep and ignores wildcard/default fallback handling inside the nested branch, so legal nested Copy-payload matches can still lower to generated `panic(
)` instead of the correct arm body.

issues.description: The fix is not actually recursive over all accepted nested built-in patterns. `extract_inner_builtin_pat` only unwraps a single inner built-in layer (`lib/codegen.ml:2383-2388`), and every nested binding site only accepts the exact shape `PatTuple (_, _, [ PatTuple (_, _, [ PatBind name ]) ])` (`lib/codegen.ml:2434`, `2486`, `2566`, `2613`, `2629`, `2670`, `2721`, `2739`). Deeper accepted Copy-payload patterns such as `Option::Some(Option::Some(Option::Some(v)))` therefore still generate `panic(
)` instead of recursively binding `v`. Separately, the nested helpers only look for explicit inner `Some`/`None` or `Ok`/`Err` arms and otherwise emit `panic(

)` (`lib/codegen.ml:2415-2451`, `2547-2638`), so valid matches like `Option::Some(Option::Some(v)) => ...; _ => ...` or `Result::Ok(Option::Some(v)) => ...; _ => ...` still miscompile even though `bind_pattern` remains recursively permissive for built-in patterns (`lib/typecheck.ml:1451-1475`). That leaves remaining accepted nested Copy-payload matches semantically broken after the fix.

summary: Fail. `d8704f6` fixes the original flat-only binding bug for the tested one-level nested Copy-payload cases, but the new helpers still do not cover all accepted nested built-in matches: deeper nesting and nested-branch fallback-to-default cases can still lower to generated panic paths instead of the correct Go arm logic.
